issues: Headline is fabricated — no headline is visible in the OCR text; the article begins directly with 'Staff Writer' and no bold headline is present.

The long description does not mention that Nanaimo goaltender Wade Flaherty faced 52 Sockeye shots, a notable detail.

The long description credits 'a strong third-period performance' vaguely but omits the specific sequence: Clarke's power-play tying goal at 1:43 of the third, then Phillips' go-ahead goal, then Tomlinson, McCormick, and Rutledge (empty-netter) sealing it — the draft does mention the scorers but the context of the ejection and power-play is missing.
The draft headline 'Sockeyes Withstand Nanaimo Pressure, Win Game 3' is not supported by the OCR — no headline text is present.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes overcame a 3-2 third-period deficit — and the ejection of captain Trevor Dickie — to defeat the Nanaimo Clippers 7-3 in Game 3 of their BCJHL semi-final series, taking a 3-0 series lead before 1,415 fans at Civic Arena.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes rallied from a 3-2 deficit entering the third period to beat the Nanaimo Clippers 7-3 on February 27, 1987 in Game 3 of their best-of-seven BCJHL semi-final at Nanaimo's Civic Arena, giving Richmond a 3-0 series lead. A disputed last-second second-period goal by Corky Parkes resulted in the ejection of Sockeyes captain Trevor Dickie, but Richmond responded on the power play: Rob Clarke tied the game at 1:43 of the third, and Jason Phillips quickly put the Sockeyes ahead for good, with Dave Tomlinson, Mike McCormick, and Dean Rutledge (empty-netter) adding to the lead. Nanaimo goaltender Wade Flaherty stopped a remarkable 52 of 59 shots, while coach Orland Kurtenbach credited his team's composure after the controversial call; Game 4 was scheduled for the following night at Civic Arena.
review.notes: No headline is visible in the OCR — the article opens directly with "Staff Writer." The fabricated headline in the draft should be cleared. All other factual elements (score, date, venue, attendance, scorers, Dickie ejection, Parkes goal, shot count) are verifiable from the OCR.
